spa_name: The Mandara Spa at Sea — Norwegian Luna
overview: The onboard spa is operated by Mandara Spa, NCL's full-service spa partner. Located on the main ship (not within the Haven), it's accessible to all guests but Haven guests receive preferred booking access. The spa includes treatment rooms, a thermal suite complex, salon, and fitness center.

holly_booked_treatments.description: A premium facial treatment combining deep cleansing, exfoliation, extractions (if needed), a customized masque, and hydrating finish. The 'Ultimate' level typically includes advanced techniques like microdermabrasion, ultrasound, or LED light therapy depending on the spa's current menu. Designed to leave skin deeply refreshed and glowing.
what_to_expect: Consultation with aesthetician at start — mention any skin sensitivities
Full face and often décolletage treatment
Relaxation table in a private treatment room
Product recommendations at end — feel free to decline sales pressure politely
tips: Arrive 30 minutes before your appointment — fill out health form and relax in thermal suite beforehand
Skip heavy makeup the day of — let your skin breathe
Don't wax or use harsh exfoliants 48 hours before the facial
You can use the thermal suite before and after your treatment (included with treatment on treatment day)
Let the aesthetician know it's your anniversary — they often add a small touch
holly_booked_treatments.gf_notes: Most skincare products are applied topically, not ingested, so gluten in products is not a concern. However, if Holly has any gluten skin sensitivity, mention it — oat-based products contain gluten.

holly_booked_treatments.time: Afternoon (confirm exact time — leave at least 1 hour gap after facial)
holly_booked_treatments.description: A contrast therapy manicure and pedicure. 'Fire' elements warm and stimulate (warm paraffin wax, warm stone massage) while 'Ice' elements cool and refresh (cooling masks, cooling massage). Leaves hands and feet exceptionally soft and polished.
what_to_expect: Warm paraffin wax application on feet
Manicure with warm paraffin on hands
Gel or regular polish finish (confirm when booking — gel lasts longer for the cruise)
Completed in the spa's nail area — may be partially open to other clients
tips: Request gel polish if available — it will last the entire cruise without chipping
Bring your own polish color if you have a preference (OPI, Essie, etc.) — spa polish selection varies
Wear open-toed shoes or flip flops from the ship so wet nails don't get smudged
Book back-to-back with the facial if possible — spend the whole morning/afternoon at the spa for a full spa day
The pedicure chairs are massage chairs — enjoy

thermal_suite.name: Thermal Suite
thermal_suite.overview: The thermal suite is a self-guided wellness complex within the spa. Unlike individual treatments, the thermal suite requires a day pass or week pass and can be visited multiple times. It is not exclusive to Haven guests — all guests with a pass may use it — but it is generally less crowded than the main pools.

passes.week_pass: ~$149-179 per person for the full cruise (best value if planning multiple visits)
tips: Week passes often go on sale on embarkation day — check the spa promotions desk when boarding
Haven guests may receive a discount — ask the concierge before purchasing
The thermal suite is included on the day of any spa treatment — useful on April 5

amenities.description: Smooth stone heated loungers — these are the most in-demand feature. Arrive early (before 9am) to claim one. Heated to approximately body temperature, they're perfect for reading or napping. The heat has a mild therapeutic effect on muscles.

Warm seawater pool with jet therapy stations targeting different muscle groups — neck, back, legs. Usually heated to ~102-104°F (39-40°C). Excellent for post-sea-day muscle recovery. Views sometimes available depending on ship design.
Traditional dry sauna, typically 170-190°F (77-88°C). 10-15 minute sessions recommended. Excellent for detox and deep muscle relaxation. Rinse in the shower between sauna and steam room.

Wet steam room infused with eucalyptus or other aromatherapy scents, typically 110-120°F (43-49°C). Lower temperature than sauna but the steam makes it feel more intense. Great for clearing sinuses and skin hydration.

A cold contrast room — some ships have a snow room that generates snowflakes, others have an ice wall or cold chamber. Used between hot sessions for circulation benefits. Jarring but invigorating — try it at least once.
Cold water showers or plunge pool for contrast therapy. 30 seconds of cold water after a sauna session dramatically improves the experience and circulation.
Quiet room adjacent to the thermal suite with reclined chairs, dim lighting, and infused water. Often has warm blankets. Zero talking rule in most spas — a genuine quiet retreat.

best_times: Early morning (7:30–9am) — least crowded, heated loungers available
During port days — most guests are ashore, thermal suite is nearly empty
Avoid: mid-morning on sea days when it gets busy (10am–noon)
etiquette: Bring flip flops — required footwear in most areas
Bathing suits required — not a clothing-optional spa
Towels provided at the spa desk
No phones or cameras in the thermal suite
Silence expected in relaxation rooms and heated lounger area
Rinse in shower before entering pools and between heat/cold cycles
Hydrate — bring a water bottle or use the infused water station
Limit sauna/steam to 15-20 minute sessions, then cool down

additional_treatments.description: Smooth basalt stones heated to ~130°F used in combination with traditional massage. The heat penetrates deeper than hands alone, making it exceptional for muscle tension. A romantic couple's option for a sea day.
additional_treatments.recommendation: Book as a couple's massage for a sea day — one of the most memorable cruise experiences.

additional_treatments.description: Side-by-side massage in a couples suite. Swedish or deep tissue options. Haven guests can sometimes request this be set up with champagne or Champagne arranged through the butler.
additional_treatments.recommendation: Ask the Haven concierge if a couples treatment can be arranged with suite amenities (rose petals, champagne) for anniversary.

additional_treatments.description: Full-body exfoliation followed by a mineral or seaweed wrap application, then cocooned in heated blankets. Deeply hydrating — skin feels exceptional afterward.
additional_treatments.recommendation: A good option for the second sea day (April 10) if spa time is desired on the last full day.

additional_treatments.description: Shorter, targeted facial using Elemis products — good option for a mid-week treatment without committing to a full-length session.
additional_treatments.recommendation: Good secondary option if the Ultimate Treatment Facial results inspire more spa time.

additional_treatments.description: Full-service salon on the ship for blowouts, updos, color touch-ups, and styling. Good for the anniversary evening look.
additional_treatments.recommendation: Consider a blowout the afternoon of April 5 before the Rocket Man show — makes the anniversary evening feel especially special.

booking_tips: Book treatments as soon as you board — April 5 is already booked, but other days fill up fast
The spa offers discounts during port days (when fewer guests are onboard) — ask at the desk
Embarkation day (April 4) often has spa open house promotions — check when boarding
Haven concierge can assist with spa reservations if the spa desk has a line
Confirm your April 5 appointment times at the spa desk on embarkation day
Read treatment descriptions carefully — some treatments include add-ons at extra cost, easy to accidentally agree to
location: Main ship — accessible from all guest areas. Not within the Haven but close to Haven elevator banks.
